speech_text: Welcome to our presentation on the final assignment, generated by DeepSlide. Today, we will discuss our proposed method for efficient nearest neighbor search.

slide_content: Stage 1: Fast Approaching • Start from routing vectors in one partition • Navigate sparse graph [公式:G_i] toward query [公式:q] • Stop when distance stops decreasing significantly • Output: candidate set [公式:C_i] from partition [公式:i] 5mm Stage 2: Precise Search • For each candidate [公式:c C_i], examine its routing neighbors • These neighbors belong to other partitions • Expand search across partitions using routing connections • Final result: nearest neighbors from entire dataset 5mm Algorithm Comp...
speech_text: Now let me introduce our proposed method called CSPG, which stands for Cross-Partition Sparse Proximity Graph. The core idea is quite elegant: we randomly partition the dataset into multiple groups, with each group sharing some common vectors called routing vectors, shown here in red. For each partition, we build a sparser and smaller proximity graph. This sparsity is key - it allows us to take larger steps when we're far from the query, enabling faster initial approach. The routing vectors act ...

slide_content: • Efficiency: Sparse graphs reduce distance computations • Scalability: Partitioning enables parallel processing • Accuracy: Cross-partition expansion ensures high recall • Flexibility: Works with various graph construction methods 5mm Key Innovations: • Random partitioning with shared routing vectors • Two-stage search: fast approaching + precise expansion • Sparse graphs for large-step navigation • Cross-partition connectivity via routing neighbors 5mm Next: We'll evaluate CSPG against state-o...
speech_text: The CSPG method employs a two-stage search algorithm. In Stage 1, we perform fast approaching. We start from the routing vectors in just one partition and navigate its sparse graph toward the query. We continue until the distance stops decreasing significantly, which gives us a candidate set from that partition. This stage is very efficient because we're working with a sparse graph that allows large jumps. In Stage 2, we perform precise search. For each candidate from Stage 1, we examine its rou...

slide_content: • Novel Partitioning Strategy: CSPG randomly partitions datasets to create smaller, sparser proximity graphs • Efficient Two-Stage Search: Combines fast proximity search with cross-partition precise expansion • Balanced Performance: Achieves optimal trade-off between speed (QPS) and accuracy (Recall@10) • Practical Improvements: Demonstrates consistent performance gains across multiple datasets and graph algorithms 0.5cm Core Innovation: Partitioning reduces distance computations while maintaini...
speech_text: To summarize, CSPG offers several key advantages. First, it's efficient - sparse graphs dramatically reduce the number of distance computations needed. Second, it's scalable - the partitioning naturally enables parallel processing across partitions. Third, it maintains high accuracy through cross-partition expansion. And fourth, it's flexible - it can work with various underlying graph construction methods. The key innovations include random partitioning with shared routing vectors, the two-stag...

slide_content: • CSPG achieves higher QPS \& Recall@10 than traditional proximity graphs • 1.5x speedup for Vamana and HCNNG across all datasets • Pushes Pareto frontier outward: Better speed without sacrificing accuracy
speech_text: In conclusion, our CSPG method makes several key contributions to approximate nearest neighbor search. First, we introduce a novel partitioning strategy that randomly divides datasets to create smaller, sparser proximity graphs. Second, we design an efficient two-stage search approach that combines fast proximity search with cross-partition precise expansion. This allows us to achieve an optimal balance between speed, measured as queries per second or QPS, and accuracy, measured as Recall@10. Mo...

slide_content: Key Contributions: • Novel partitioning strategy creates smaller, sparser graphs • Efficient two-stage search: fast proximity + cross-partition expansion • Balances speed (QPS) and accuracy (Recall@10) 0.3cm Experimental Results: • Higher QPS \& Recall@10 than traditional proximity graphs • 1.5x speedup for Vamana and HCNNG • Validates partitioning reduces computations while maintaining quality
speech_text: Let me show you the experimental results that validate our approach. This figure from ANN-Benchmarks shows the QPS versus Recall@10 performance curve for the SIFT1M dataset. You can see that CSPG consistently achieves higher QPS and higher Recall@10 compared to traditional proximity graph implementations. For algorithms like Vamana and HCNNG, we achieve at least 1.5x speedup across all datasets. The key takeaway is that CSPG pushes the Pareto frontier outward, meaning we get better speed without...

In conclusion, CSPG makes key contributions to approximate nearest neighbor search through a novel partitioning strategy that creates smaller, sparser graphs and an efficient two-stage search combining fast proximity search with cross-partition expansion. This approach achieves an optimal balance between speed, measured as QPS, and accuracy, measured as Recall@10.
